title: Network Inversion for Uncertainty-Aware Out-of-Distribution Detection

problem.statement: Modern neural networks tend to produce overconfident predictions even on inputs outside the training distribution, making it crucial to develop models capable of both out-of-distribution (OOD) detection and uncertainty estimation (UE).
problem.importance: These capabilities are vital for trustworthiness in deployment scenarios where data encountered during inference may deviate from the training distribution.

limitations_of_existing_approaches: Most existing approaches treat OOD detection and UE separately
Often rely on post-hoc calibration techniques or auxiliary OOD datasets

related_work.name: Autoinverse
related_work.description: Framework for neural network inversion that prioritizes solutions near reliable training samples

related_work.name: SCOOD
related_work.description: Semantically coherent OOD detection approach combining uncertainty-aware optimal transport

related_work.name: Gaussian process-based model
related_work.description: Operates solely on in-distribution data, defining predictive uncertainty scores without requiring OOD examples

related_work.name: PostNet
related_work.description: Employs normalizing flows to model posterior distributions over predicted probabilities

proposed_approach.name: Novel framework leveraging network inversion
key_features: Detects OOD inputs and estimates prediction uncertainty
Unifies both objectives in a single training procedure
Extends a standard (n+1)-class model with an auxiliary garbage class
Iteratively refines the model using inverted reconstructions
Encourages the network to carve out clean decision boundaries while isolating ambiguous or anomalous regions
advantages: Requires no external OOD datasets or post-hoc calibration
Offers a simple and interpretable solution for robustness in classification under distributional shift

training_approach.description: Integrates OOD detection and UE into a single framework using network inversion and an auxiliary garbage class
training_approach.implementation: For an n-class classification task, extends to an (n+1)-class model by introducing an additional 'garbage' class

garbage_class.initialization: Initially populated with random Gaussian noise, representing unstructured inputs not observed in the training distribution

network_inversion_process.process: Reconstructs samples from the input space of the classifier for all output classes
network_inversion_process.early_stage_behavior: Early reconstructions tend to be visually incoherent and don't resemble real data, reflecting the model's incomplete or uncertain understanding of the class manifolds
network_inversion_process.assignment: These reconstructions are assigned to the garbage class and added to the training set for subsequent epochs
network_inversion_process.loss_function: Trained using a weighted cross-entropy loss
